message.content:
Implement the following plan:

# Admin Login + Full KPI & Plan CRUD + Employee-Scoped Calculation

## Context

The Commission app has role-based personas (persona switcher in Header), a read-only KPI Library page, a Plan Builder with mostly read-only tabs, and a calculation page that doesn't let you pick an employee. The user wants an end-to-end admin workflow: log in as admin → manage KPIs → configure plans with all 8 tabs editable → calculate commissions for a specific employee + month.

---

## 1. Admin Login

**Header.jsx** — Replace persona switcher with an "Admin" default persona that has level 6 (sees executive dashboard). Keep the ability to "view as" other employees for testing.

- Default persona: `{ id: 'admin', name: 'Admin', role: 'Administrator', roleId: 'role-nsm' }`
- The persona dropdown stays so admin can preview dashboards as any role
- `store.js` default → admin persona

---

## 2. KPI Library — Full CRUD

### Backend: `server/src/routes/kpis.js`
Add:
- `PUT /kpis/:id` — Update KPI fields
- `DELETE /kpis/:id` — Soft-delete (set is_active=0)

### Frontend: `client/src/pages/KpiLibraryPage.jsx`
- **"+ New KPI" button** in header → opens modal form
- **Edit button** on each KPI card → opens same modal pre-filled
- **Delete button** on each card → confirm then soft-delete
- **KPI Form modal** with fields: name, code, category, description, formula, unit, direction, applicable_roles (multi-select)
- After create/edit/delete → refresh list

---

## 3. Plan Builder — Full Editing on All 8 Tabs

### Backend: `server/src/routes/plans.js`
Existing endpoints: POST plan, PUT plan, PUT roles, PUT territories, PUT kpis.

Add 7 new endpoints:
- `PUT /plans/:id/slabs` — body: `{ slab_sets: [{ name, type, kpi_id, tiers: [{...}] }] }`
- `PUT /plans/:id/rules` — body: `{ rule_sets: [{ name, description, rules: [{...}] }] }`
- `PUT /plans/:id/eligibility` — body: `{ rules: [{metric, operator, threshold, action, reduction_percent}] }`
- `PUT /plans/:id/multipliers` — body: `{ rules: [{name, type, condition_metric, ...}] }`
- `PUT /plans/:id/penalties` — body: `{ rules: [{name, trigger_metric, ...}] }`
- `PUT /plans/:id/caps` — body: `{ rules: [{cap_type, cap_value}] }`
- `PUT /plans/:id/splits` — body: `{ rules: [{name, participants: [{role_id, split_percent}]}] }`

Each endpoint: delete existing → insert new (full replace pattern, same as existing kpis endpoint).

### Frontend: `client/src/pages/PlanBuilderPage.jsx`

Every tab becomes editable with inline forms. Each tab has its own Save button.

**General Tab** — Wire up Save button:
- For new plans: POST → redirect to `/plans/:newId`
- For existing: PUT plan + PUT roles + PUT territories

**KPIs Tab** — Add/remove/edit:
- Dropdown to pick KPI from library + "Add" button
- Inline editable: target_value (number), weight (number)
- Remove button per row
- Save → PUT /plans/:id/kpis

**Slabs Tab** — Add/remove/edit slab sets and tiers:
- "Add Slab Set" → name, type dropdown, KPI dropdown
- Per set: "Add Tier" → min%, max%, rate, rate_type
- Delete tier / delete set buttons
- Save → PUT /plans/:id/slabs

**Rules Tab** — Add/remove/edit:
- "Add Rule Set" → name, description
- Per set: "Add Rule" → dimension, rule_type, match_type, match_values (comma-separated)
- Save → PUT /plans/:id/rules

**Eligibility Tab** — Add/remove/edit:
- "Add Rule" → metric, operator, threshold, action, reduction%
- Save → PUT /plans/:id/eligibility

**Multipliers Tab** — Add/remove/edit:
- "Add Multiplier" → name, type, condition_metric, operator, value, multiplier_value, stacking
- Save → PUT /plans/:id/multipliers

**Penalties Tab** — Add/remove/edit:
- "Add Penalty" → name, trigger_metric, operator, value, penalty_type, penalty_value
- Save → PUT /plans/:id/penalties

**Caps & Splits Tab** — Add/remove/edit:
- "Add Cap" → cap_type, cap_value
- "Add Split" → name, + participant rows (role, percent)
- Save → PUT /plans/:id/caps + PUT /plans/:id/splits

---

## 4. Calculation Page — Employee Selection

### Frontend: `client/src/pages/CalculationPage.jsx`
- Add employee dropdown (fetched from `/employees`): "All Employees" + individual employees
- Fix `created_by` to `'admin'` instead of hardcoded `emp-01`

### Backend: `server/src/engine/calculationPipeline.js`
- Accept optional `employee_id` parameter
- When provided, filter `filteredEmployees` to just that one employee

---

## 5. Files Changed

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `server/src/routes/kpis.js` | Add PUT, DELETE endpoints |
| `server/src/routes/plans.js` | Add 7 new PUT endpoints |
| `server/src/routes/calculation.js` | Pass employee_id to pipeline |
| `server/src/engine/calculationPipeline.js` | Filter by employee_id |
| `client/src/pages/KpiLibraryPage.jsx` | Full CRUD with modal form |
| `client/src/pages/PlanBuilderPage.jsx` | All 8 tabs editable with save |
| `client/src/pages/CalculationPage.jsx` | Employee dropdown, fix created_by |
| `client/src/components/layout/Header.jsx` | Admin default persona |
| `client/src/store/store.js` | Admin default |

---

## 6. Verification

1. App opens as Admin, sees executive dashboard
2. KPI Library: Create → Edit → Delete all work
3. Plan Builder: Edit every tab → save → reload → changes persist
4. Create new plan from scratch with all config
5. Calculate: Select plan + specific employee + Jan 2026 → results for that employee
6. Calculate: Select plan + "All" + Jan 2026 → results for all salesmen


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/fci/.claude/projects/-Users-fci-Desktop-Prakash-Commission/3baacb19-6ee2-405a-8e21-07497aa3561a.jsonl

export function createSchema(db) {
  db.exec(`
    -- =============================================
    -- REFERENCE TABLES
    -- =============================================

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS roles (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
      level INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      description TEXT,
      is_field_role INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS territories (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      name TEXT NOT NULL,
      type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(type IN ('national','region','area','territory')),
      parent_id TEXT REFERENCES territories(id),
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS employees (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      name TEXT NOT NULL,
      email TEXT NOT NULL,
      role_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES roles(id),
      territory_id TEXT REFERENCES territories(id),
      reports_to TEXT REFERENCES employees(id),
      base_salary REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      hire_date TEXT NOT NULL,
      is_active INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS products (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      name TEXT NOT NULL,
      sku TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
      category TEXT NOT NULL,
      subcategory TEXT,
      unit_price REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      is_strategic INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      is_new_launch INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      tags TEXT DEFAULT '[]',
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS customers (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      name TEXT NOT NULL,
      channel TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(channel IN ('GT','MT','KA','Wholesale','Online')),
      territory_id TEXT REFERENCES territories(id),
      credit_limit REAL DEFAULT 0,
      tags TEXT DEFAULT '[]',
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    );

    -- =============================================
    -- KPI DEFINITIONS
    -- =============================================

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS kpi_definitions (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      name TEXT NOT NULL,
      code TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
      category TEXT NOT NULL,
      description TEXT,
      formula TEXT NOT NULL,
      unit TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'currency',
      direction TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'higher_is_better' CHECK(direction IN ('higher_is_better','lower_is_better')),
      applicable_roles TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
      is_active INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    );

    -- =============================================
    -- COMMISSION PLANS
    -- =============================================

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS commission_plans (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      name TEXT NOT NULL,
      description TEXT,
      status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft' CHECK(status IN ('draft','active','expired','archived')),
      plan_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'monthly' CHECK(plan_type IN ('monthly','quarterly','annual')),
      effective_from TEXT NOT NULL,
      effective_to TEXT NOT NULL,
      base_payout REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      created_by TEXT,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS plan_roles (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      plan_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      role_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES roles(id),
      UNIQUE(plan_id, role_id)
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS plan_territories (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      plan_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      territory_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES territories(id),
      UNIQUE(plan_id, territory_id)
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS plan_kpis (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      plan_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      kpi_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES kpi_definitions(id),
      weight REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      target_value REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      slab_set_id TEXT REFERENCES slab_sets(id),
      UNIQUE(plan_id, kpi_id)
    );

    -- =============================================
    -- SLAB CONFIGURATION
    -- =============================================

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS slab_sets (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      name TEXT NOT NULL,
      type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'step' CHECK(type IN ('step','progressive','accelerator')),
      plan_id TEXT REFERENCES commission_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      kpi_id TEXT REFERENCES kpi_definitions(id),
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS slab_tiers (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      slab_set_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES slab_sets(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      tier_order INTEGER NOT NULL,
      min_percent REAL NOT NULL,
      max_percent REAL,
      rate REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      rate_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'percentage' CHECK(rate_type IN ('percentage','fixed','per_unit')),
      UNIQUE(slab_set_id, tier_order)
    );

    -- =============================================
    -- RULE ENGINE
    -- =============================================

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS rule_sets (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      plan_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      name TEXT NOT NULL,
      description TEXT,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS rules (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      rule_set_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES rule_sets(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      dimension TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(dimension IN ('product','customer','product_category','customer_channel')),
      rule_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(rule_type IN ('include','exclude')),
      match_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'exact' CHECK(match_type IN ('exact','category','tag')),
      match_values TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
      priority INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS eligibility_rules (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      plan_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      metric TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(metric IN ('min_sales','min_collection_percent','max_return_percent','min_active_days','min_lines_sold')),
      operator TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '>=' CHECK(operator IN ('>=','<=','>','<','=')),
      threshold REAL NOT NULL,
      action TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'zero_payout' CHECK(action IN ('zero_payout','reduce_percent','warning_only')),
      reduction_percent REAL DEFAULT 0,
      is_active INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS multiplier_rules (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      plan_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      name TEXT NOT NULL,
      type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(type IN ('growth','strategic_sku','new_launch','channel_mix','collection_speed')),
      condition_metric TEXT NOT NULL,
      condition_operator TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '>=' CHECK(condition_operator IN ('>=','<=','>','<','=')),
      condition_value REAL NOT NULL,
      multiplier_value REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 1.0,
      stacking_mode TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'multiplicative' CHECK(stacking_mode IN ('additive','multiplicative','highest_only')),
      is_active INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS penalty_rules (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      plan_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      name TEXT NOT NULL,
      trigger_metric TEXT NOT NULL,
      trigger_operator TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '>' CHECK(trigger_operator IN ('>=','<=','>','<','=')),
      trigger_value REAL NOT NULL,
      penalty_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'percentage' CHECK(penalty_type IN ('percentage','fixed','slab_downgrade')),
      penalty_value REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      is_active INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS capping_rules (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      plan_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      cap_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(cap_type IN ('max_per_plan','percent_of_salary','max_per_kpi')),
      cap_value REAL NOT NULL,
      is_active INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS split_rules (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      plan_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      name TEXT NOT NULL,
      trigger_condition TEXT,
      is_active INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS split_participants (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      split_rule_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES split_rules(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      role_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES roles(id),
      split_percent REAL NOT NULL,
      UNIQUE(split_rule_id, role_id)
    );

    -- =============================================
    -- TRANSACTIONS
    -- =============================================

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transactions (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      employee_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES employees(id),
      customer_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES customers(id),
      product_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES products(id),
      transaction_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'sale' CHECK(transaction_type IN ('sale','return','collection')),
      quantity REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      amount REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      transaction_date TEXT NOT NULL,
      period TEXT NOT NULL,
      territory_id TEXT REFERENCES territories(id),
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    );

    -- =============================================
    -- CALCULATION RESULTS
    -- =============================================

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS calculation_runs (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      plan_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
      period TEXT NOT NULL,
      status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'running' CHECK(status IN ('running','completed','failed','locked')),
      is_simulation INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      simulation_params TEXT,
      total_payout REAL DEFAULT 0,
      employee_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
      started_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
      completed_at TEXT,
      calculation_details TEXT DEFAULT '{}',
      created_by TEXT
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS employee_payouts (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      run_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES calculation_runs(id),
      employee_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES employees(id),
      plan_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
      period TEXT NOT NULL,
      gross_payout REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      multiplier_amount REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      penalty_amount REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      cap_adjustment REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      split_adjustment REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      net_payout REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      eligibility_status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'eligible',
      eligibility_details TEXT DEFAULT '{}',
      calculation_details TEXT DEFAULT '{}',
      approval_status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK(approval_status IN ('pending','submitted','manager_approved','finance_approved','hr_approved','rejected','locked')),
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS kpi_results (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      payout_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES employee_payouts(id),
      kpi_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES kpi_definitions(id),
      target_value REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      actual_value REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      achievement_percent REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      slab_rate REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      slab_type TEXT,
      raw_payout REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      weighted_payout REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      weight REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      calculation_details TEXT DEFAULT '{}'
    );

    -- =============================================
    -- WORKFLOW & AUDIT
    -- =============================================

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS approval_log (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      payout_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES employee_payouts(id),
      action TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(action IN ('submitted','manager_approved','finance_approved','hr_approved','rejected','locked')),
      acted_by TEXT NOT NULL,
      acted_by_role TEXT,
      comments TEXT,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_trail (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      entity_type TEXT NOT NULL,
      entity_id TEXT NOT NULL,
      action TEXT NOT NULL,
      changes TEXT DEFAULT '{}',
      performed_by TEXT,
      performed_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS simulation_snapshots (
      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      run_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES calculation_runs(id),
      name TEXT,
      params TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
      results TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
      created_by TEXT,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    );

    -- =============================================
    -- INDEXES
    -- =============================================

    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_employees_role ON employees(role_id);
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_employees_territory ON employees(territory_id);
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_transactions_employee ON transactions(employee_id);
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_transactions_period ON transactions(period);
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_transactions_type ON transactions(transaction_type);
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_plan_kpis_plan ON plan_kpis(plan_id);
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_slab_tiers_set ON slab_tiers(slab_set_id);
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_rules_set ON rules(rule_set_id);
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_employee_payouts_run ON employee_payouts(run_id);
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_employee_payouts_employee ON employee_payouts(employee_id);
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_kpi_results_payout ON kpi_results(payout_id);
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_approval_log_payout ON approval_log(payout_id);
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_trail_entity ON audit_trail(entity_type, entity_id);
  `);
}

content.text:
All 9 files have been updated. Here's a summary of changes:

**Backend (4 files):**

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `server/src/routes/kpis.js` | Added `PUT /:id` (update KPI) and `DELETE /:id` (soft-delete via `is_active=0`) |
| `server/src/routes/plans.js` | Added 7 new PUT endpoints: `/slabs`, `/rules`, `/eligibility`, `/multipliers`, `/penalties`, `/caps`, `/splits` — all use delete-then-insert pattern |
| `server/src/routes/calculation.js` | Passes `employee_id` from request body to pipeline |
| `server/src/engine/calculationPipeline.js` | Accepts `employee_id` param, filters `filteredEmployees` to single employee when provided |

**Frontend (5 files):**

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `client/src/store/store.js` | Default persona → Admin (role-nsm) |
| `client/src/components/layout/Header.jsx` | Admin added as first persona in dropdown |
| `client/src/pages/KpiLibraryPage.jsx` | Full CRUD: "+New KPI" button, Edit/Delete buttons per card, modal form with all fields |
| `client/src/pages/PlanBuilderPage.jsx` | All 8 tabs fully editable with inline forms + Save button per tab. General tab wires up create/update. New plan → POST then redirect |
| `client/src/pages/CalculationPage.jsx` | Employee dropdown (from `/employees`), `created_by` changed to `'admin'` |

content.thinking:
The user is asking about the formula field in the KPI form - they're questioning how users would know what to write there. This is a good UX question. The formula field is currently a free-text input where users type things like `SUM(test_values)` or `SUM(transactions.amount) WHERE type=sale`. 

Let me first finish the current testing verification, then address their question.

Looking at the screenshot, I can see "Test KPI" was created successfully with code "TEST_KPI_CODE" and formula "SUM(test_values)" in the Revenue category. The KPI CRUD is working.

Let me address the user's question about formula UX. The formulas in the existing KPIs are descriptive/pseudo-code strings that describe how the KPI is calculated - they're not actually executed. Looking at the calculation engine, the KPI achievement is calculated by `calculateKpiAchievement` which uses the KPI code to determine what to compute, not the formula string. The formula field is essentially documentation/metadata.


So the formula field is really just a descriptive label that documents the calculation approach - it's not actually executed as code. The user's concern is valid though, since it's not immediately clear that this is just documentation rather than something that needs to be syntactically correct. We could definitely improve this with helper text or a builder interface to guide users.

content.text:
Good question. The "Test KPI" was created successfully (visible in the Revenue filter with code `TEST_KPI_CODE`), so CRUD works. But your point about the formula UX is valid.

Looking at the existing KPIs and the calculation engine (`server/src/engine/step03_kpiAchievement.js`), the `formula` field is **descriptive metadata** — it's a human-readable label that documents how the KPI value is derived. The actual calculation logic lives in the engine code and uses the KPI `code` to decide what to compute (e.g., `TOTAL_REVENUE` → sum sales amounts). The formula string itself is never parsed or executed.

So currently it's just documentation, like:
- `SUM(transactions.amount) WHERE type=sale`
- `(SUM(amount WHERE type=collection) / SUM(amount WHERE type=sale)) * 100`

That said, a free-text input with no guidance isn't great UX. Some options to improve it:

1. **Add placeholder examples + helper text** — show "Describe how this KPI is calculated" with examples below the field
2. **Dropdown of common formula templates** — predefined patterns like "SUM of sales", "COUNT of customers", "Ratio A/B" that users select and customize
3. **Visual formula builder** — a step-by-step builder with dropdowns for metric, aggregation, filters

Would you like me to implement one of these improvements, or is the current descriptive approach fine for now and you'd rather I continue testing the other features (Plan Builder tabs, Calculation page employee picker)?

message.content: What's the best way a great commission solution should have?

content.thinking:
The user is asking a product/design question about how a great commission solution should handle KPI formula configuration. Let me think about this from a product perspective, looking at how real commission/incentive compensation management (ICM) tools handle this.

The best commission solutions (like Xactly, CaptivateIQ, Varicent, Spiff, etc.) typically handle formulas/KPI configuration in a few ways:

1. **CaptivateIQ approach**: Spreadsheet-like formula builder where you reference data sources with a familiar Excel-like syntax. Users can write formulas like `=SUM(Deals.Amount WHERE Deals.Stage = "Closed Won")`.


2. **Spiff approach**: Visual/no-code builder with drag-and-drop components. You build rules by selecting from dropdowns: "When [metric] [operator] [value] then [payout logic]".
3. **Xactly approach**: Template-based with pre-built calculation components that admins configure through a wizard UI.

For this Commission app specifically, I think the best approach would be a structured formula builder that references actual data columns from the transactions table and provides predefined aggregation functions like SUM and COUNT, rather than allowing free-text input.

But looking at the codebase more carefully, I realize the formulas aren't actually executed—the KPI calculation is hardcoded in `step03_kpiAchievement.js` based on the KPI code. So the real architectural question is whether to build a no-code formula builder that connects to the data model, lets admins define KPIs visually through data sources and filters, and then compiles and executes those formulas against transaction data instead of relying on hardcoded logic.

content.text: Great product question. Let me look at how the formula is actually used in the engine before suggesting the right approach.

This confirms the issue. Right now:

- The `formula` field is **dead text** — purely cosmetic documentation
- The actual logic is a **hardcoded switch/case** on `kpi_code` in `step03_kpiAchievement.js`
- If you create a new KPI like "Test KPI" with code `TEST_KPI_CODE`, it hits `default: actual = 0` — it will never actually compute anything

Here's how the best commission platforms handle this:

**Tier 1 — Structured formula builder (CaptivateIQ / Spiff style):**
The admin picks building blocks from dropdowns — no free text at all:

```
Metric:      [Transaction Amount ▼]
Aggregation: [SUM ▼]
Filter:      [Transaction Type ▼] [equals ▼] [sale ▼]
             + Add filter
```

The system stores this as structured JSON and the engine evaluates it dynamically. No hardcoded switch/case needed. This is the gold standard because:
- Zero code knowledge required
- New KPIs work instantly without developer changes
- Impossible to write an invalid formula
- Engine can validate & preview results

**Tier 2 — Expression DSL (Xactly style):**
A mini-language like `SUM(amount) WHERE type='sale' AND product.is_strategic=1` that the engine actually parses and executes. More powerful but requires admin training.

**For this app, I'd recommend Tier 1** — a structured formula builder that replaces both the free-text formula field AND the hardcoded switch/case. The engine would dynamically evaluate the stored JSON config.

Want me to implement this? It would involve:

1. Replace the formula text input with a visual builder (metric + aggregation + filters)
2. Store the formula as structured JSON in the DB
3. Rewrite `step03_kpiAchievement.js` to dynamically evaluate the JSON formula against transactions
4. Any new KPI created through the UI would immediately work in calculations

message.content: Let's go with tier one, please.

content.content:
1→export function fetchScopedTransactions(db, employeeId, period, territoryId) {
     2→  const transactions = db.prepare(`
     3→    SELECT t.*, p.name as product_name, p.category as product_category, p.sku,
     4→           p.is_strategic, p.is_new_launch, p.tags as product_tags,
     5→           c.name as customer_name, c.channel as customer_channel, c.tags as customer_tags
     6→    FROM transactions t
     7→    JOIN products p ON t.product_id = p.id
     8→    JOIN customers c ON t.customer_id = c.id
     9→    WHERE t.employee_id = ? AND t.period = ?
    10→    ORDER BY t.transaction_date
    11→  `).all(employeeId, period);
    12→  
    13→  return transactions;
    14→}
    15→

content.content:
1→export function createSchema(db) {
     2→  db.exec(`
     3→    -- =============================================
     4→    -- REFERENCE TABLES
     5→    -- =============================================
     6→
     7→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS roles (
     8→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
     9→      name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    10→      level INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    11→      description TEXT,
    12→      is_field_role INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    13→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    14→    );
    15→
    16→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS territories (
    17→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    18→      name TEXT NOT NULL,
    19→      type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(type IN ('national','region','area','territory')),
    20→      parent_id TEXT REFERENCES territories(id),
    21→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    22→    );
    23→
    24→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS employees (
    25→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    26→      name TEXT NOT NULL,
    27→      email TEXT NOT NULL,
    28→      role_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES roles(id),
    29→      territory_id TEXT REFERENCES territories(id),
    30→      reports_to TEXT REFERENCES employees(id),
    31→      base_salary REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    32→      hire_date TEXT NOT NULL,
    33→      is_active INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    34→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    35→    );
    36→
    37→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS products (
    38→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    39→      name TEXT NOT NULL,
    40→      sku TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    41→      category TEXT NOT NULL,
    42→      subcategory TEXT,
    43→      unit_price REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    44→      is_strategic INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    45→      is_new_launch INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    46→      tags TEXT DEFAULT '[]',
    47→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    48→    );
    49→
    50→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS customers (
    51→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    52→      name TEXT NOT NULL,
    53→      channel TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(channel IN ('GT','MT','KA','Wholesale','Online')),
    54→      territory_id TEXT REFERENCES territories(id),
    55→      credit_limit REAL DEFAULT 0,
    56→      tags TEXT DEFAULT '[]',
    57→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    58→    );
    59→
    60→    -- =============================================
    61→    -- KPI DEFINITIONS
    62→    -- =============================================
    63→
    64→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS kpi_definitions (
    65→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    66→      name TEXT NOT NULL,
    67→      code TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    68→      category TEXT NOT NULL,
    69→      description TEXT,
    70→      formula TEXT NOT NULL,
    71→      unit TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'currency',
    72→      direction TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'higher_is_better' CHECK(direction IN ('higher_is_better','lower_is_better')),
    73→      applicable_roles TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
    74→      is_active INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    75→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    76→    );
    77→
    78→    -- =============================================
    79→    -- COMMISSION PLANS
    80→    -- =============================================
    81→
    82→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS commission_plans (
    83→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    84→      name TEXT NOT NULL,
    85→      description TEXT,
    86→      status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft' CHECK(status IN ('draft','active','expired','archived')),
    87→      plan_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'monthly' CHECK(plan_type IN ('monthly','quarterly','annual')),
    88→      effective_from TEXT NOT NULL,
    89→      effective_to TEXT NOT NULL,
    90→      base_payout REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    91→      created_by TEXT,
    92→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    93→      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    94→    );
    95→
    96→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS plan_roles (
    97→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    98→      plan_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    99→      role_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES roles(id),
   100→      UNIQUE(plan_id, role_id)
   101→    );
   102→
   103→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS plan_territories (
   104→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   105→      plan_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   106→      territory_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES territories(id),
   107→      UNIQUE(plan_id, territory_id)
   108→    );
   109→
   110→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS plan_kpis (
   111→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   112→      plan_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   113→      kpi_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES kpi_definitions(id),
   114→      weight REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   115→      target_value REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   116→      slab_set_id TEXT REFERENCES slab_sets(id),
   117→      UNIQUE(plan_id, kpi_id)
   118→    );
   119→
   120→    -- =============================================
   121→    -- SLAB CONFIGURATION
   122→    -- =============================================
   123→
   124→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS slab_sets (
   125→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   126→      name TEXT NOT NULL,
   127→      type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'step' CHECK(type IN ('step','progressive','accelerator')),
   128→      plan_id TEXT REFERENCES commission_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   129→      kpi_id TEXT REFERENCES kpi_definitions(id),
   130→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   131→    );
   132→
   133→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS slab_tiers (
   134→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   135→      slab_set_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES slab_sets(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   136→      tier_order INTEGER NOT NULL,
   137→      min_percent REAL NOT NULL,
   138→      max_percent REAL,
   139→      rate REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   140→      rate_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'percentage' CHECK(rate_type IN ('percentage','fixed','per_unit')),
   141→      UNIQUE(slab_set_id, tier_order)
   142→    );
   143→
   144→    -- =============================================
   145→    -- RULE ENGINE
   146→    -- =============================================
   147→
   148→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS rule_sets (
   149→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   150→      plan_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   151→      name TEXT NOT NULL,
   152→      description TEXT,
   153→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   154→    );
   155→
   156→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS rules (
   157→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   158→      rule_set_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES rule_sets(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   159→      dimension TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(dimension IN ('product','customer','product_category','customer_channel')),
   160→      rule_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(rule_type IN ('include','exclude')),
   161→      match_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'exact' CHECK(match_type IN ('exact','category','tag')),
   162→      match_values TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
   163→      priority INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   164→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   165→    );
   166→
   167→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS eligibility_rules (
   168→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   169→      plan_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   170→      metric TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(metric IN ('min_sales','min_collection_percent','max_return_percent','min_active_days','min_lines_sold')),
   171→      operator TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '>=' CHECK(operator IN ('>=','<=','>','<','=')),
   172→      threshold REAL NOT NULL,
   173→      action TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'zero_payout' CHECK(action IN ('zero_payout','reduce_percent','warning_only')),
   174→      reduction_percent REAL DEFAULT 0,
   175→      is_active INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
   176→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   177→    );
   178→
   179→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS multiplier_rules (
   180→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   181→      plan_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   182→      name TEXT NOT NULL,
   183→      type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(type IN ('growth','strategic_sku','new_launch','channel_mix','collection_speed')),
   184→      condition_metric TEXT NOT NULL,
   185→      condition_operator TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '>=' CHECK(condition_operator IN ('>=','<=','>','<','=')),
   186→      condition_value REAL NOT NULL,
   187→      multiplier_value REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 1.0,
   188→      stacking_mode TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'multiplicative' CHECK(stacking_mode IN ('additive','multiplicative','highest_only')),
   189→      is_active INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
   190→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   191→    );
   192→
   193→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS penalty_rules (
   194→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   195→      plan_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   196→      name TEXT NOT NULL,
   197→      trigger_metric TEXT NOT NULL,
   198→      trigger_operator TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '>' CHECK(trigger_operator IN ('>=','<=','>','<','=')),
   199→      trigger_value REAL NOT NULL,
   200→      penalty_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'percentage' CHECK(penalty_type IN ('percentage','fixed','slab_downgrade')),
   201→      penalty_value REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   202→      is_active INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
   203→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   204→    );
   205→
   206→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS capping_rules (
   207→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   208→      plan_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   209→      cap_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(cap_type IN ('max_per_plan','percent_of_salary','max_per_kpi')),
   210→      cap_value REAL NOT NULL,
   211→      is_active INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
   212→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   213→    );
   214→
   215→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS split_rules (
   216→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   217→      plan_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   218→      name TEXT NOT NULL,
   219→      trigger_condition TEXT,
   220→      is_active INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
   221→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   222→    );
   223→
   224→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS split_participants (
   225→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   226→      split_rule_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES split_rules(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   227→      role_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES roles(id),
   228→      split_percent REAL NOT NULL,
   229→      UNIQUE(split_rule_id, role_id)
   230→    );
   231→
   232→    -- =============================================
   233→    -- TRANSACTIONS
   234→    -- =============================================
   235→
   236→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transactions (
   237→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   238→      employee_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES employees(id),
   239→      customer_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES customers(id),
   240→      product_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES products(id),
   241→      transaction_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'sale' CHECK(transaction_type IN ('sale','return','collection')),
   242→      quantity REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   243→      amount REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   244→      transaction_date TEXT NOT NULL,
   245→      period TEXT NOT NULL,
   246→      territory_id TEXT REFERENCES territories(id),
   247→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   248→    );
   249→
   250→    -- =============================================
   251→    -- CALCULATION RESULTS
   252→    -- =============================================
   253→
   254→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS calculation_runs (
   255→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   256→      plan_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
   257→      period TEXT NOT NULL,
   258→      status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'running' CHECK(status IN ('running','completed','failed','locked')),
   259→      is_simulation INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   260→      simulation_params TEXT,
   261→      total_payout REAL DEFAULT 0,
   262→      employee_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   263→      started_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
   264→      completed_at TEXT,
   265→      calculation_details TEXT DEFAULT '{}',
   266→      created_by TEXT
   267→    );
   268→
   269→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS employee_payouts (
   270→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   271→      run_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES calculation_runs(id),
   272→      employee_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES employees(id),
   273→      plan_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES commission_plans(id),
   274→      period TEXT NOT NULL,
   275→      gross_payout REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   276→      multiplier_amount REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   277→      penalty_amount REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   278→      cap_adjustment REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   279→      split_adjustment REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   280→      net_payout REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   281→      eligibility_status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'eligible',
   282→      eligibility_details TEXT DEFAULT '{}',
   283→      calculation_details TEXT DEFAULT '{}',
   284→      approval_status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK(approval_status IN ('pending','submitted','manager_approved','finance_approved','hr_approved','rejected','locked')),
   285→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   286→    );
   287→
   288→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS kpi_results (
   289→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   290→      payout_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES employee_payouts(id),
   291→      kpi_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES kpi_definitions(id),
   292→      target_value REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   293→      actual_value REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   294→      achievement_percent REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   295→      slab_rate REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   296→      slab_type TEXT,
   297→      raw_payout REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   298→      weighted_payout REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   299→      weight REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   300→      calculation_details TEXT DEFAULT '{}'
   301→    );
   302→
   303→    -- =============================================
   304→    -- WORKFLOW & AUDIT
   305→    -- =============================================
   306→
   307→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS approval_log (
   308→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   309→      payout_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES employee_payouts(id),
   310→      action TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(action IN ('submitted','manager_approved','finance_approved','hr_approved','rejected','locked')),
   311→      acted_by TEXT NOT NULL,
   312→      acted_by_role TEXT,
   313→      comments TEXT,
   314→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   315→    );
   316→
   317→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_trail (
   318→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   319→      entity_type TEXT NOT NULL,
   320→      entity_id TEXT NOT NULL,
   321→      action TEXT NOT NULL,
   322→      changes TEXT DEFAULT '{}',
   323→      performed_by TEXT,
   324→      performed_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   325→    );
   326→
   327→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS simulation_snapshots (
   328→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   329→      run_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES calculation_runs(id),
   330→      name TEXT,
   331→      params TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
   332→      results TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
   333→      created_by TEXT,
   334→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   335→    );
   336→
   337→    -- =============================================
   338→    -- INDEXES
   339→    -- =============================================
   340→
   341→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_employees_role ON employees(role_id);
   342→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_employees_territory ON employees(territory_id);
   343→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_transactions_employee ON transactions(employee_id);
   344→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_transactions_period ON transactions(period);
   345→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_transactions_type ON transactions(transaction_type);
   346→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_plan_kpis_plan ON plan_kpis(plan_id);
   347→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_slab_tiers_set ON slab_tiers(slab_set_id);
   348→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_rules_set ON rules(rule_set_id);
   349→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_employee_payouts_run ON employee_payouts(run_id);
   350→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_employee_payouts_employee ON employee_payouts(employee_id);
   351→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_kpi_results_payout ON kpi_results(payout_id);
   352→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_approval_log_payout ON approval_log(payout_id);
   353→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_audit_trail_entity ON audit_trail(entity_type, entity_id);
   354→  `);
   355→}

   141→    // Volume
   142→    { id: 'kpi-03', name: 'Units Sold', code: 'UNITS_SOLD', category: 'Volume', description: 'Total units sold in the period', formula: 'SUM(transactions.quantity) WHERE type=sale', unit: 'number', direction: 'higher_is_better', applicable_roles: '["role-sr","role-psr","role-de"]', is_active: 1 },
   143→    // Distribution

   145→    { id: 'kpi-05', name: 'Lines Per Call', code: 'LINES_PER_CALL', category: 'Distribution', description: 'Average product lines sold per customer visit', formula: 'COUNT(DISTINCT product_id) / COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id)', unit: 'number', direction: 'higher_is_better', applicable_roles: '["role-sr","role-psr"]', is_active: 1 },
   146→    // Collection

   148→    // Returns

   153→    // Customer

   329→  // ==================== TRANSACTIONS (~500) ====================
   330→  const txns = [];
   331→  const period = '2026-01';
   332→  const prevPeriod = '2025-01';
   333→
   334→  // Helper to generate transactions for an employee
   335→  function genSales(empId, custIds, prodIds, terrId, totalTarget, achievePercent, opts = {}) {
   336→    const { returnPercent = 3, collectionPercent = 90, strategicBias = false } = opts;
   337→    const totalAmount = totalTarget * (achievePercent / 100);
   338→    const txnCount = Math.floor(15 + Math.random() * 20);
   339→    const amtPerTxn = totalAmount / txnCount;
   340→
   341→    for (let i = 0; i < txnCount; i++) {
   342→      const custId = custIds[Math.floor(Math.random() * custIds.length)];
   343→      let prodId = prodIds[Math.floor(Math.random() * prodIds.length)];
   344→      // Bias toward strategic products if needed
   345→      if (strategicBias && Math.random() < 0.4) {
   346→        const strategicProds = prodIds.filter(p => ['prod-01', 'prod-03', 'prod-07', 'prod-10', 'prod-13'].includes(p));
   347→        if (strategicProds.length) prodId = strategicProds[Math.floor(Math.random() * strategicProds.length)];
   348→      }
   349→      const prod = products.find(p => p.id === prodId);
   350→      const amount = amtPerTxn * (0.7 + Math.random() * 0.6);
   351→      const qty = Math.max(1, Math.round(amount / (prod?.unit_price || 50)));
   352→      const day = String(Math.floor(1 + Math.random() * 28)).padStart(2, '0');
   353→
   354→      txns.push({
   355→        id: uuid(),
   356→        employee_id: empId,
   357→        customer_id: custId,
   358→        product_id: prodId,
   359→        transaction_type: 'sale',
   360→        quantity: qty,
   361→        amount: Math.round(amount * 100) / 100,
   362→        transaction_date: `2026-01-${day}`,
   363→        period,
   364→        territory_id: terrId,
   365→      });
   366→    }
   367→
   368→    // Returns
   369→    const returnAmount = totalAmount * (returnPercent / 100);
   370→    const returnCount = Math.max(1, Math.floor(txnCount * 0.1));
   371→    for (let i = 0; i < returnCount; i++) {
   372→      const custId = custIds[Math.floor(Math.random() * custIds.length)];
   373→      const prodId = prodIds[Math.floor(Math.random() * prodIds.length)];
   374→      const prod = products.find(p => p.id === prodId);
   375→      const amt = (returnAmount / returnCount) * (0.8 + Math.random() * 0.4);
   376→      const qty = Math.max(1, Math.round(amt / (prod?.unit_price || 50)));
   377→      const day = String(Math.floor(5 + Math.random() * 23)).padStart(2, '0');
   378→
   379→      txns.push({
   380→        id: uuid(),
   381→        employee_id: empId,
   382→        customer_id: custId,
   383→        product_id: prodId,
   384→        transaction_type: 'return',
   385→        quantity: qty,
   386→        amount: Math.round(amt * 100) / 100,
   387→        transaction_date: `2026-01-${day}`,
   388→        period,
   389→        territory_id: terrId,
   390→      });
   391→    }
   392→
   393→    // Collections
   394→    const collectionAmount = totalAmount * (collectionPercent / 100);
   395→    const collCount = Math.max(1, Math.floor(txnCount * 0.5));
   396→    for (let i = 0; i < collCount; i++) {
   397→      const custId = custIds[Math.floor(Math.random() * custIds.length)];
   398→      const prodId = prodIds[0]; // Collection linked to primary product
   399→      const amt = (collectionAmount / collCount) * (0.8 + Math.random() * 0.4);
   400→      const day = String(Math.floor(10 + Math.random() * 18)).padStart(2, '0');
   401→
   402→      txns.push({
   403→        id: uuid(),
   404→        employee_id: empId,
   405→        customer_id: custId,
   406→        product_id: prodId,
   407→        transaction_type: 'collection',
   408→        quantity: 0,
   409→        amount: Math.round(amt * 100) / 100,
   410→        transaction_date: `2026-01-${day}`,
   411→        period,
   412→        territory_id: terrId,
   413→      });
   414→    }
   415→
   416→    // Previous period (for growth calculations) - roughly 80% of current
   417→    const prevAmount = totalAmount * 0.8;
   418→    const prevTxnCount = Math.floor(txnCount * 0.7);
   419→    for (let i = 0; i < prevTxnCount; i++) {
   420→      const custId = custIds[Math.floor(Math.random() * custIds.length)];
   421→      const prodId = prodIds[Math.floor(Math.random() * prodIds.length)];
   422→      const prod = products.find(p => p.id === prodId);
   423→      const amount = (prevAmount / prevTxnCount) * (0.7 + Math.random() * 0.6);
   424→      const qty = Math.max(1, Math.round(amount / (prod?.unit_price || 50)));
   425→
   426→      txns.push({
   427→        id: uuid(),
   428→        employee_id: empId,
   429→        customer_id: custId,
   430→        product_id: prodId,
   431→        transaction_type: 'sale',
   432→        quantity: qty,
   433→        amount: Math.round(amount * 100) / 100,
   434→        transaction_date: `2025-01-${String(Math.floor(1 + Math.random() * 28)).padStart(2, '0')}`,
   435→        period: prevPeriod,
   436→        territory_id: terrId,
   437→      });
   438→    }
   439→  }
   440→
   441→  const delhiProducts = ['prod-01', 'prod-02', 'prod-03', 'prod-05', 'prod-06', 'prod-07', 'prod-09', 'prod-10', 'prod-13', 'prod-17'];
   442→  const mumbaiProducts = ['prod-01', 'prod-02', 'prod-05', 'prod-08', 'prod-09', 'prod-11', 'prod-14', 'prod-15', 'prod-18', 'prod-19'];
   443→  const chennaiProducts = ['prod-01', 'prod-04', 'prod-06', 'prod-09', 'prod-10', 'prod-12', 'prod-14', 'prod-16', 'prod-17', 'prod-20'];
   444→  const delhiCustomers = ['cust-01', 'cust-02', 'cust-07', 'cust-09', 'cust-14'];
   445→  const mumbaiCustomers = ['cust-03', 'cust-04', 'cust-08', 'cust-10', 'cust-15'];
   446→  const chennaiCustomers = ['cust-05', 'cust-06', 'cust-11'];
   447→  const kaCustomers = ['cust-12', 'cust-13'];
   448→
   449→  // emp-06 (Amit, SR Delhi) - 110% achiever (tests accelerator)
   450→  genSales('emp-06', delhiCustomers, delhiProducts, 'terr-delhi', 500000, 110, { strategicBias: true, returnPercent: 4, collectionPercent: 92 });
   451→
   452→  // emp-07 (Neha, SR Delhi) - 95% achiever (solid mid-performer)
   453→  genSales('emp-07', delhiCustomers, delhiProducts, 'terr-delhi', 500000, 95, { returnPercent: 3, collectionPercent: 88 });
   454→
   455→  // emp-08 (Ravi, SR Mumbai) - 125% achiever (star, tests high slab)
   456→  genSales('emp-08', mumbaiCustomers, mumbaiProducts, 'terr-mumbai', 500000, 125, { strategicBias: true, returnPercent: 2, collectionPercent: 95 });
   457→
   458→  // emp-09 (Lakshmi, SR Chennai) - 55% achiever (below threshold, tests eligibility)
   459→  genSales('emp-09', chennaiCustomers, chennaiProducts, 'terr-chennai', 500000, 55, { returnPercent: 5, collectionPercent: 55 });
   460→
   461→  // emp-10 (Deepak, PSR Delhi) - 100% achiever
   462→  genSales('emp-10', delhiCustomers, delhiProducts, 'terr-delhi', 300000, 100, { returnPercent: 3, collectionPercent: 85 });
   463→
   464→  // emp-11 (Manoj, DE Delhi) - 90% achiever
   465→  genSales('emp-11', delhiCustomers, delhiProducts, 'terr-delhi', 200000, 90, { returnPercent: 2, collectionPercent: 92 });
   466→
   467→  // emp-12 (Kavita, KAM) - 105% but HIGH returns (tests penalty)
   468→  genSales('emp-12', kaCustomers, [...delhiProducts, ...mumbaiProducts], 'terr-nat', 800000, 105, { returnPercent: 12, collectionPercent: 80 });
   469→
   470→  console.log(`Generated ${txns.length} transactions`);
   471→  insert('transactions', txns);
   472→
   473→  // ==================== AUDIT TRAIL ====================
   474→  const auditEntries = [
   475→    { id: uuid(), entity_type: 'plan', entity_id: 'plan-01', action: 'created', changes: JSON.stringify({ name: 'Field Sales Monthly Incentive', status: 'draft' }), performed_by: 'emp-01' },
   476→    { id: uuid(), entity_type: 'plan', entity_id: 'plan-01', action: 'activated', changes: JSON.stringify({ status: { from: 'draft', to: 'active' } }), performed_by: 'emp-01' },
   477→    { id: uuid(), entity_type: 'plan', entity_id: 'plan-02', action: 'created', changes: JSON.stringify({ name: 'Supervisor Quarterly Bonus', status: 'draft' }), performed_by: 'emp-01' },
   478→    { id: uuid(), entity_type: 'plan', entity_id: 'plan-02', action: 'activated', changes: JSON.stringify({ status: { from: 'draft', to: 'active' } }), performed_by: 'emp-01' },
   479→  ];
   480→  insert('audit_trail', auditEntries);
   481→
   482→  console.log('Seed complete!');
   483→  console.log(`  Roles: ${roles.length}`);
   484→  console.log(`  Territories: ${territories.length}`);
   485→  console.log(`  Products: ${products.length}`);
   486→  console.log(`  Customers: ${customers.length}`);
   487→  console.log(`  Employees: ${employees.length}`);
   488→  console.log(`  KPIs: ${kpis.length}`);
   489→  console.log(`  Plans: ${plans.length}`);
   490→  console.log(`  Transactions: ${txns.length}`);
   491→}
   492→
   493→seed();
   494→

content.text:
Perfect! I have all the information you need. Let me compile a comprehensive analysis of the data model for KPI formulas.

## Full Data Model for KPI Formulas

### 1. Transaction Object Structure at Step03

When transactions reach `step03_kpiAchievement.js`, they have the following fields (from the JOIN in step01):

**Direct Transaction Fields:**
- `id` - Primary key
- `employee_id` - Reference to employee
- `customer_id` - Reference to customer
- `product_id` - Reference to product
- `transaction_type` - 'sale', 'return', or 'collection'
- `quantity` - Numeric quantity sold/returned
- `amount` - Monetary value
- `transaction_date` - Date of transaction
- `period` - YYYY-MM format for grouping

**Joined Product Fields:**
- `product_id` (already exists)
- `product_name` - Text name
- `product_category` - Text category (Beverages, Snacks, Dairy, Personal Care, Home Care)
- `sku` - Stock keeping unit
- `is_strategic` - Boolean/Integer (1 or 0) - marks strategic SKUs
- `is_new_launch` - Boolean/Integer (1 or 0) - marks new launch products
- `product_tags` - JSON array of tags (e.g., ["carbonated","flagship"])

**Joined Customer Fields:**
- `customer_id` (already exists)
- `customer_name` - Text name
- `customer_channel` - Text channel (GT, MT, KA, Wholesale, Online)
- `customer_tags` - JSON array of tags (e.g., ["regular"], ["premium"], ["chain"])

### 2. KPI Calculation Patterns in Step03

The code uses these aggregation patterns:

**Simple SUM Aggregations:**
```javascript
// TOTAL_REVENUE: SUM all amounts where transaction_type === 'sale'
sales.reduce((sum, t) => sum + t.amount, 0)

// UNITS_SOLD: SUM all quantities where transaction_type === 'sale'
sales.reduce((sum, t) => sum + t.quantity, 0)
```

**COUNT DISTINCT Aggregations:**
```javascript
// OUTLET_COVERAGE: COUNT unique customer_ids in sales
new Set(sales.map(t => t.customer_id)).size

// NEW_CUSTOMERS: COUNT unique customer_ids
new Set(sales.map(t => t.customer_id)).size
```

**Ratio/Percentage Aggregations:**
```javascript
// COLLECTION_PERCENT: (collection_total / sales_total) * 100
(totalCollections / totalSales) * 100

// RETURN_PERCENT: (return_total / sales_total) * 100
(totalReturns / totalSalesAmt) * 100

// LINES_PER_CALL: unique_products / unique_customers
uniqueProducts / uniqueCustomers
```

**Filtered Aggregations (with attribute filters):**
```javascript
// STRATEGIC_SKU_REV: SUM amounts where is_strategic === 1 AND type === 'sale'
sales.filter(t => t.is_strategic).reduce((sum, t) => sum + t.amount, 0)

// NEW_LAUNCH_SALES: SUM amounts where is_new_launch === 1 AND type === 'sale'
sales.filter(t => t.is_new_launch).reduce((sum, t) => sum + t.amount, 0)
```

**Derived Metrics:**
```javascript
// REV_PER_OUTLET: total_revenue / unique_customers
revTotal / outlets

// REVENUE_GROWTH: ((current - previous_year_same_month) / previous) * 100
// Requires DB query to fetch previous period data
```

**Team Aggregations (manager-level):**
```javascript
// TEAM_REVENUE: SUM of all direct reports' revenues (requires recursive lookups)
// TEAM_TARGET_ACH: AVG of all direct reports' achievement percentages
```

### 3. Filter Dimensions (Mapping/Filtering)

From `step02_mappingFilters.js`, transactions can be filtered on these dimensions:

```javascript
switch (rule.dimension) {
  case 'product':              // Exact product IDs
    matchValues.includes(txn.product_id)
    
  case 'customer':             // Exact customer IDs
    matchValues.includes(txn.customer_id)
    
  case 'product_category':     // Category name (e.g., "Beverages")
    matchValues.includes(txn.product_category)
    
  case 'customer_channel':     // Channel type (GT, MT, KA, Wholesale, Online)
    matchValues.includes(txn.customer_channel)
}
```

Each rule has:
- `rule_type` - 'include' or 'exclude'
- `match_type` - 'exact', 'category', or 'tag'
- `match_values` - JSON array of values to match

Rules are applied with **inclusion/exclusion logic**:
- Include rules must match (if any include rules exist)
- Exclude rules override (act as blockers)

### 4. Seeded Test Data Shape

From `seed.js`, transactions include:

**Product Attributes Present in Data:**
- Products with `is_strategic = 1`: Cola Classic, Energy Boost, Protein Bar, Greek Yogurt, Shampoo Premium
- Products with `is_new_launch = 1`: Energy Boost, Protein Bar, Probiotic Drink
- Products with tags: "carbonated", "flagship", "energy", "juice", "new", "chips", "health", "premium", etc.

**Customer Channels:**
- GT (General Trade): Small kirana stores - 8 customers
- MT (Modern Trade): Hypermarkets/chains - 3 customers
- KA (Key Accounts): National accounts - 2 customers
- Wholesale: Distribution hubs - 2 customers

**Typical Transaction Mix per Employee:**
- 15-20 sales transactions per month
- 1-2 returns (3-12% of sales amount)
- 50% of sales count as collection transactions
- Previous year data (~70-80% of current)

### 5. Computed Fields & Enrichments Before Step03

**From Step01 (fetchTransactions):**
- Joins products table to get `is_strategic`, `is_new_launch`, product attributes
- Joins customers table to get `customer_channel` 
- Filters to employee_id and period

**From Step02 (applyMappingFilters):**
- Filters transactions based on rule sets (product, customer, category, channel dimensions)
- No fields added, just subset of transactions returned

**No computed fields are pre-calculated** - all aggregations happen in Step03 within each KPI case statement.

### 6. Key Patterns for Formula Development

When building new KPI formulas, you can use:

**Available Operations:**
- `.filter(t => condition)` - subset transactions
- `.reduce((sum, t) => sum + t.field, 0)` - aggregate
- `new Set(txn.map(t => t.field)).size` - count distinct
- Direct comparisons with boolean/integer fields (`t.is_strategic`, `t.is_new_launch`)
- Ratio calculations: `(value1 / value2) * 100`

**Available Filter Conditions:**
- `t.transaction_type === 'sale'|'return'|'collection'`
- `t.is_strategic === 1|0`
- `t.is_new_launch === 1|0`
- `t.customer_channel === 'GT'|'MT'|'KA'|'Wholesale'|'Online'`
- `t.product_category === 'Beverages'|'Snacks'|...`
- Numeric comparisons: `t.amount > X`, `t.quantity >= Y`

**Database Access Available:**
- For cross-period comparisons (previous year revenue, team member data)
- Query pattern: `db.prepare(sql).get(param)` or `.all(params)`

This gives you the complete picture of what data is available and how it flows through the KPI calculation engine.

- `t.customer_channel === 'GT'|'MT'|'KA'|'Wholesale'|'Online'`
- `t.product_category === 'Beverages'|'Snacks'|...`
- Numeric comparisons: `t.amount > X`, `t.quantity >= Y`

**Database Access Available:**
- For cross-period comparisons (previous year revenue, team member data)
- Query pattern: `db.prepare(sql).get(param)` or `.all(params)`

This gives you the complete picture of what data is available and how it flows through the KPI calculation engine.

input.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for replacing the free-text KPI formula field with a Tier 1 structured visual formula builder, and making the calculation engine dynamically evaluate it.

## Context

**Current state:**
- `formula` field in `kpi_definitions` table is a free-text string (dead metadata)
- `step03_kpiAchievement.js` has a hardcoded switch/case on `kpi_code` for 15 KPIs
- New KPIs created via the UI hit `default: actual = 0` — they never compute

**Goal:** Replace with a structured formula builder so any KPI created through the UI works automatically in calculations.

## Available Transaction Fields at Step03

When transactions reach step03, each has these fields (from JOIN in step01):
- `transaction_type` — 'sale' | 'return' | 'collection'
- `amount` — monetary value
- `quantity` — units
- `product_id`, `product_name`, `product_category`, `sku`
- `is_strategic` — 0/1
- `is_new_launch` — 0/1
- `product_tags` — JSON array
- `customer_id`, `customer_name`, `customer_channel` — GT/MT/KA/Wholesale/Online
- `customer_tags` — JSON array
- `territory_id`, `transaction_date`, `period`

## Existing KPI Patterns to Support

From step03's switch/case:
1. **Simple SUM**: `TOTAL_REVENUE` = SUM(amount) WHERE type=sale
2. **Filtered SUM**: `STRATEGIC_SKU_REV` = SUM(amount) WHERE type=sale AND is_strategic=1
3. **SUM quantity**: `UNITS_SOLD` = SUM(quantity) WHERE type=sale
4. **COUNT DISTINCT**: `OUTLET_COVERAGE` = COUNT_DISTINCT(customer_id) WHERE type=sale
5. **Ratio**: `COLLECTION_PERCENT` = (SUM(amount) WHERE type=collection / SUM(amount) WHERE type=sale) * 100
6. **Ratio**: `RETURN_PERCENT` = (SUM(amount) WHERE type=return / SUM(amount) WHERE type=sale) * 100
7. **Computed ratio**: `LINES_PER_CALL` = COUNT_DISTINCT(product_id) / COUNT_DISTINCT(customer_id) WHERE type=sale
8. **Revenue per outlet**: `REV_PER_OUTLET` = SUM(amount) / COUNT_DISTINCT(customer_id) WHERE type=sale
9. **Growth**: `REVENUE_GROWTH` = growth vs previous year (requires DB query)
10. **Team metrics**: `TEAM_REVENUE` = SUM of direct reports' revenue (requires DB query)

## Design Requirements

### Formula JSON Schema
Design a structured JSON format stored in `kpi_definitions.formula` (replacing the free-text). It should represent:
- Aggregation function (SUM, COUNT_DISTINCT, AVG)
- Field to aggregate (amount, quantity, customer_id, product_id)
- Transaction type filter (sale, return, collection, or all)
- Optional attribute filters (is_strategic, is_new_launch, product_category, customer_channel)
- For ratios: numerator + denominator each with their own aggregation/filters
- For special cases (growth, team): a `type` field that flags special handling

### UI Builder
Replace the formula text input in KpiLibraryPage modal with dropdowns:
- **Formula Type**: Simple (single aggregation) | Ratio (A/B) | Growth | Team
- For Simple: Aggregation dropdown + Field dropdown + Transaction Type + optional filters
- For Ratio: Same as Simple but for numerator AND denominator, auto-multiply by 100
- For Growth: base metric config + "vs previous year" flag
- For Team: base metric config + "aggregate reports" flag
- Show a live preview text of what the formula computes

### Engine Changes
- Rewrite `step03_kpiAchievement.js` to parse the JSON formula and evaluate dynamically
- Keep switch/case as fallback for legacy KPIs that still have text formulas
- Growth and Team remain special-cased (need DB access) but configured via structured JSON

### Migration
- Update the 15 existing seed KPIs to use the new JSON formula format
- Ensure backward compatibility: if formula is a string (old format), fall through to legacy switch/case

## UI Patterns to Follow (from existing codebase)
- CSS classes: `.input`, `.label`, `.btn-primary`, `.badge`, `.card`
- Icons from lucide-react
- `cn()` utility for conditional classes
- Toast notifications via react-hot-toast
- Inline forms like PlanBuilder tabs (not separate pages)

Please design:
1. The exact JSON schema for formulas
2. The UI component structure
3. The engine evaluation logic
4. The seed data migration

content.text:
Design a detailed implementation plan for replacing the free-text KPI formula field with a Tier 1 structured visual formula builder, and making the calculation engine dynamically evaluate it.

## Context

**Current state:**
- `formula` field in `kpi_definitions` table is a free-text string (dead metadata)
- `step03_kpiAchievement.js` has a hardcoded switch/case on `kpi_code` for 15 KPIs
- New KPIs created via the UI hit `default: actual = 0` — they never compute

**Goal:** Replace with a structured formula builder so any KPI created through the UI works automatically in calculations.

## Available Transaction Fields at Step03

When transactions reach step03, each has these fields (from JOIN in step01):
- `transaction_type` — 'sale' | 'return' | 'collection'
- `amount` — monetary value
- `quantity` — units
- `product_id`, `product_name`, `product_category`, `sku`
- `is_strategic` — 0/1
- `is_new_launch` — 0/1
- `product_tags` — JSON array
- `customer_id`, `customer_name`, `customer_channel` — GT/MT/KA/Wholesale/Online
- `customer_tags` — JSON array
- `territory_id`, `transaction_date`, `period`

## Existing KPI Patterns to Support

From step03's switch/case:
1. **Simple SUM**: `TOTAL_REVENUE` = SUM(amount) WHERE type=sale
2. **Filtered SUM**: `STRATEGIC_SKU_REV` = SUM(amount) WHERE type=sale AND is_strategic=1
3. **SUM quantity**: `UNITS_SOLD` = SUM(quantity) WHERE type=sale
4. **COUNT DISTINCT**: `OUTLET_COVERAGE` = COUNT_DISTINCT(customer_id) WHERE type=sale
5. **Ratio**: `COLLECTION_PERCENT` = (SUM(amount) WHERE type=collection / SUM(amount) WHERE type=sale) * 100
6. **Ratio**: `RETURN_PERCENT` = (SUM(amount) WHERE type=return / SUM(amount) WHERE type=sale) * 100
7. **Computed ratio**: `LINES_PER_CALL` = COUNT_DISTINCT(product_id) / COUNT_DISTINCT(customer_id) WHERE type=sale
8. **Revenue per outlet**: `REV_PER_OUTLET` = SUM(amount) / COUNT_DISTINCT(customer_id) WHERE type=sale
9. **Growth**: `REVENUE_GROWTH` = growth vs previous year (requires DB query)
10. **Team metrics**: `TEAM_REVENUE` = SUM of direct reports' revenue (requires DB query)

## Design Requirements

### Formula JSON Schema
Design a structured JSON format stored in `kpi_definitions.formula` (replacing the free-text). It should represent:
- Aggregation function (SUM, COUNT_DISTINCT, AVG)
- Field to aggregate (amount, quantity, customer_id, product_id)
- Transaction type filter (sale, return, collection, or all)
- Optional attribute filters (is_strategic, is_new_launch, product_category, customer_channel)
- For ratios: numerator + denominator each with their own aggregation/filters
- For special cases (growth, team): a `type` field that flags special handling

### UI Builder
Replace the formula text input in KpiLibraryPage modal with dropdowns:
- **Formula Type**: Simple (single aggregation) | Ratio (A/B) | Growth | Team
- For Simple: Aggregation dropdown + Field dropdown + Transaction Type + optional filters
- For Ratio: Same as Simple but for numerator AND denominator, auto-multiply by 100
- For Growth: base metric config + "vs previous year" flag
- For Team: base metric config + "aggregate reports" flag
- Show a live preview text of what the formula computes

### Engine Changes
- Rewrite `step03_kpiAchievement.js` to parse the JSON formula and evaluate dynamically
- Keep switch/case as fallback for legacy KPIs that still have text formulas
- Growth and Team remain special-cased (need DB access) but configured via structured JSON

### Migration
- Update the 15 existing seed KPIs to use the new JSON formula format
- Ensure backward compatibility: if formula is a string (old format), fall through to legacy switch/case

## UI Patterns to Follow (from existing codebase)
- CSS classes: `.input`, `.label`, `.btn-primary`, `.badge`, `.card`
- Icons from lucide-react
- `cn()` utility for conditional classes
- Toast notifications via react-hot-toast
- Inline forms like PlanBuilder tabs (not separate pages)

Please design:
1. The exact JSON schema for formulas
2. The UI component structure
3. The engine evaluation logic
4. The seed data migration
5. File-by-file changes needed

8. **Revenue per outlet**: `REV_PER_OUTLET` = SUM(amount) / COUNT_DISTINCT(customer_id) WHERE type=sale
9. **Growth**: `REVENUE_GROWTH` = growth vs previous year (requires DB query)
10. **Team metrics**: `TEAM_REVENUE` = SUM of direct reports' revenue (requires DB query)

## Design Requirements

### Formula JSON Schema
Design a structured JSON format stored in `kpi_definitions.formula` (replacing the free-text). It should represent:
- Aggregation function (SUM, COUNT_DISTINCT, AVG)
- Field to aggregate (amount, quantity, customer_id, product_id)
- Transaction type filter (sale, return, collection, or all)
- Optional attribute filters (is_strategic, is_new_launch, product_category, customer_channel)
- For ratios: numerator + denominator each with their own aggregation/filters
- For special cases (growth, team): a `type` field that flags special handling

### UI Builder
Replace the formula text input in KpiLibraryPage modal with dropdowns:
- **Formula Type**: Simple (single aggregation) | Ratio (A/B) | Growth | Team
- For Simple: Aggregation dropdown + Field dropdown + Transaction Type + optional filters
- For Ratio: Same as Simple but for numerator AND denominator, auto-multiply by 100
- For Growth: base metric config + "vs previous year" flag
- For Team: base metric config + "aggregate reports" flag
- Show a live preview text of what the formula computes

### Engine Changes
- Rewrite `step03_kpiAchievement.js` to parse the JSON formula and evaluate dynamically
- Keep switch/case as fallback for legacy KPIs that still have text formulas
- Growth and Team remain special-cased (need DB access) but configured via structured JSON

### Migration
- Update the 15 existing seed KPIs to use the new JSON formula format
- Ensure backward compatibility: if formula is a string (old format), fall through to legacy switch/case

## UI Patterns to Follow (from existing codebase)
- CSS classes: `.input`, `.label`, `.btn-primary`, `.badge`, `.card`
- Icons from lucide-react
- `cn()` utility for conditional classes
- Toast notifications via react-hot-toast
- Inline forms like PlanBuilder tabs (not separate pages)

1. The exact JSON schema for formulas
2. The UI component structure
3. The engine evaluation logic
4. The seed data migration
5. File-by-file changes needed

Design a detailed implementation plan for replacing the free-text KPI formula field with a Tier 1 structured visual formula builder, and making the calculation engine dynamically evaluate it.

## Context

**Current state:**
- `formula` field in `kpi_definitions` table is a free-text string (dead metadata)
- `step03_kpiAchievement.js` has a hardcoded switch/case on `kpi_code` for 15 KPIs
- New KPIs created via the UI hit `default: actual = 0` — they never compute

**Goal:** Replace with a structured formula builder so any KPI created through the UI works automatically in calculations.

## Available Transaction Fields at Step03

When transactions reach step03, each has these fields (from JOIN in step01):
- `transaction_type` — 'sale' | 'return' | 'collection'
- `amount` — monetary value
- `quantity` — units
- `product_id`, `product_name`, `product_category`, `sku`
- `is_strategic` — 0/1
- `is_new_launch` — 0/1
- `product_tags` — JSON array
- `customer_id`, `customer_name`, `customer_channel` — GT/MT/KA/Wholesale/Online
- `customer_tags` — JSON array
- `territory_id`, `transaction_date`, `period`

From step03's switch/case:
1. **Simple SUM**: `TOTAL_REVENUE` = SUM(amount) WHERE type=sale
2. **Filtered SUM**: `STRATEGIC_SKU_REV` = SUM(amount) WHERE type=sale AND is_strategic=1
3. **SUM quantity**: `UNITS_SOLD` = SUM(quantity) WHERE type=sale

- Aggregation function (SUM, COUNT_DISTINCT, AVG)
- Field to aggregate (amount, quantity, customer_id, product_id)
- Transaction type filter (sale, return, collection, or all)
- Optional attribute filters (is_strategic, is_new_launch, product_category, customer_channel)

- For Team: base metric config + "aggregate reports" flag
- Show a live preview text of what the formula computes

- Rewrite `step03_kpiAchievement.js` to parse the JSON formula and evaluate dynamically
- Keep switch/case as fallback for legacy KPIs that still have text formulas
- Growth and Team remain special-cased (need DB access) but configured via structured JSON

### Migration
- Update the 15 existing seed KPIs to use the new JSON formula format
- Ensure backward compatibility: if formula is a string (old format), fall through to legacy switch/case

- CSS classes: `.input`, `.label`, `.btn-primary`, `.badge`, `.card`
- Icons from lucide-react
- `cn()` utility for conditional classes
- Toast notifications via react-hot-toast
- Inline forms like PlanBuilder tabs (not separate pages)

Please design:
1. The exact JSON schema for formulas
2. The UI component structure
3. The engine evaluation logic
4. The seed data migration

data.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for replacing the free-text KPI formula field with a Tier 1 structured visual formula builder, and making the calculation engine dynamically evaluate it.

## Context

**Current state:**
- `formula` field in `kpi_definitions` table is a free-text string (dead metadata)
- `step03_kpiAchievement.js` has a hardcoded switch/case on `kpi_code` for 15 KPIs
- New KPIs created via the UI hit `default: actual = 0` — they never compute

**Goal:** Replace with a structured formula builder so any KPI created through the UI works automatically in calculations.

## Available Transaction Fields at Step03

When transactions reach step03, each has these fields (from JOIN in step01):
- `transaction_type` — 'sale' | 'return' | 'collection'
- `amount` — monetary value
- `quantity` — units
- `product_id`, `product_name`, `product_category`, `sku`
- `is_strategic` — 0/1
- `is_new_launch` — 0/1
- `product_tags` — JSON array
- `customer_id`, `customer_name`, `customer_channel` — GT/MT/KA/Wholesale/Online
- `customer_tags` — JSON array
- `territory_id`, `transaction_date`, `period`

## Existing KPI Patterns to Support

From step03's switch/case:
1. **Simple SUM**: `TOTAL_REVENUE` = SUM(amount) WHERE type=sale
2. **Filtered SUM**: `STRATEGIC_SKU_REV` = SUM(amount) WHERE type=sale AND is_strategic=1
3. **SUM quantity**: `UNITS_SOLD` = SUM(quantity) WHERE type=sale
4. **COUNT DISTINCT**: `OUTLET_COVERAGE` = COUNT_DISTINCT(customer_id) WHERE type=sale
5. **Ratio**: `COLLECTION_PERCENT` = (SUM(amount) WHERE type=collection / SUM(amount) WHERE type=sale) * 100
6. **Ratio**: `RETURN_PERCENT` = (SUM(amount) WHERE type=return / SUM(amount) WHERE type=sale) * 100
7. **Computed ratio**: `LINES_PER_CALL` = COUNT_DISTINCT(product_id) / COUNT_DISTINCT(customer_id) WHERE type=sale
8. **Revenue per outlet**: `REV_PER_OUTLET` = SUM(amount) / COUNT_DISTINCT(customer_id) WHERE type=sale
9. **Growth**: `REVENUE_GROWTH` = growth vs previous year (requires DB query)
10. **Team metrics**: `TEAM_REVENUE` = SUM of direct reports' revenue (requires DB query)

## Design Requirements

### Formula JSON Schema
Design a structured JSON format stored in `kpi_definitions.formula` (replacing the free-text). It should represent:
- Aggregation function (SUM, COUNT_DISTINCT, AVG)
- Field to aggregate (amount, quantity, customer_id, product_id)
- Transaction type filter (sale, return, collection, or all)
- Optional attribute filters (is_strategic, is_new_launch, product_category, customer_channel)
- For ratios: numerator + denominator each with their own aggregation/filters
- For special cases (growth, team): a `type` field that flags special handling

### UI Builder
Replace the formula text input in KpiLibraryPage modal with dropdowns:
- **Formula Type**: Simple (single aggregation) | Ratio (A/B) | Growth | Team
- For Simple: Aggregation dropdown + Field dropdown + Transaction Type + optional filters
- For Ratio: Same as Simple but for numerator AND denominator, auto-multiply by 100
- For Growth: base metric config + "vs previous year" flag
- For Team: base metric config + "aggregate reports" flag
- Show a live preview text of what the formula computes

### Engine Changes
- Rewrite `step03_kpiAchievement.js` to parse the JSON formula and evaluate dynamically
- Keep switch/case as fallback for legacy KPIs that still have text formulas
- Growth and Team remain special-cased (need DB access) but configured via structured JSON

### Migration
- Update the 15 existing seed KPIs to use the new JSON formula format
- Ensure backward compatibility: if formula is a string (old format), fall through to legacy switch/case

## UI Patterns to Follow (from existing codebase)
- CSS classes: `.input`, `.label`, `.btn-primary`, `.badge`, `.card`
- Icons from lucide-react
- `cn()` utility for conditional classes
- Toast notifications via react-hot-toast
- Inline forms like PlanBuilder tabs (not separate pages)

Please design:
1. The exact JSON schema for formulas
2. The UI component structure
3. The engine evaluation logic
4. The seed data migration
5. File-by-file changes needed
